skill.name: interactive-portfolio
skill.description: Expert in building portfolios that actually land jobs and clients - not just showing work, but creating memorable experiences. Covers developer portfolios, designer portfolios, creative portfolios.
skill.summary: Portfolio Experience Designer that helps create memorable, conversion-focused portfolios for developers and designers.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings dismissed as false positives. The detected patterns are Markdown code block delimiters (```) and YAML frontmatter, not executable code. This skill contains only documentation content with no network access, file system operations, or system commands.

content.user_title: Build a Portfolio That Lands Jobs and Clients
content.value_statement: Transform your portfolio from a forgettable resume into a memorable experience that converts visitors into opportunities. Get proven patterns for showcasing your work effectively.
actual_capabilities: Design portfolio architecture optimized for 30-second first impressions
Structure project case studies that highlight impact and results
Create developer-focused portfolios with code quality demonstrations
Plan navigation patterns for single-page and multi-page portfolios
Craft compelling hero sections and project cards
Integrate testimonials and social proof effectively
limitations: Does not generate actual code or deploy portfolios
Focuses on design and content strategy, not technical implementation
Requires user to have their own projects and work samples ready
Does not provide hosting or domain recommendations

use_cases.title: Developer Seeking First Tech Job
use_cases.description: A bootcamp graduate or CS student needs a portfolio to showcase side projects and demonstrate coding skills to hiring managers.

use_cases.title: Designer Transitioning to Tech
use_cases.description: A visual designer wants to present their creative work professionally online with case studies that explain their design process.

use_cases.title: Freelancer Attracting Clients
use_cases.description: An independent developer or designer needs a portfolio that converts visitors into paying clients through effective CTAs and social proof.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Portfolio Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan the structure of my portfolio. I am a [your role] with [number] projects to showcase. What sections should I include and in what order?

prompt_templates.title: Project Case Study Template
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to write a case study for my [project type] project. The project [brief description]. Help me structure this as a compelling case study that shows my impact.

prompt_templates.title: Portfolio Review and Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current portfolio structure: [describe or paste your portfolio]. What improvements would you suggest to make it more memorable and conversion-focused?

prompt_templates.title: Developer Portfolio Deep Dive
prompt_templates.prompt: I am a [specialization] developer. Help me select and present my best projects for [target companies or roles]. My key projects are: [list projects]. What should I highlight for each?

output_examples.input: I'm a frontend developer with 3 projects: an e-commerce site, a weather app, and a task manager. How should I present them?
output_examples.output: Prioritize your e-commerce site first - it demonstrates real business impact. For each project, lead with the problem solved (not features built), include your specific technical contributions, show code snippets that demonstrate clean architecture decisions, and link to live demos with GitHub repositories.

output_examples.input: My portfolio has 15 projects. Should I show them all?
output_examples.output: No. Show 4-6 of your strongest projects. Quality over quantity always. Hiring managers spend about 30 seconds on portfolios - they will not review 15 projects deeply. Select projects that: solve real problems, demonstrate different skills, have measurable impact, and represent the type of work you want more of.

best_practices: Lead with your strongest project - first impressions determine whether visitors continue exploring
Use specific metrics and outcomes instead of generic feature lists (increased conversions 40 percent, not built a website)
Make contact information prominent - every portfolio section should guide visitors toward reaching out
anti_patterns: Using generic templates without personalization - your portfolio should reflect your unique style and voice
Prioritizing visual flair over project substance - impressive animations cannot compensate for weak project presentations
Including outdated or irrelevant work - remove projects that do not represent your current skills or career goals

faq.question: How many projects should I showcase in my portfolio?
faq.answer: Focus on 4 to 6 high-quality projects. This gives visitors enough depth to understand your capabilities without overwhelming them. Each project should have a detailed case study rather than just screenshots.

faq.question: Should my portfolio be a single page or multiple pages?
faq.answer: Single-page portfolios work well for designers and creatives with smooth scrolling and animations. Multi-page portfolios suit developers with extensive project documentation. A hybrid approach places main sections on one page with detailed case studies on separate pages.

faq.question: What if my projects are from tutorials or courses?
faq.answer: Tutorial projects are acceptable for beginners, but customize them significantly. Add unique features, improve the design, solve a real problem with the concept, or combine multiple tutorial concepts into one larger project that demonstrates your creativity.

faq.question: How important is the About section?
faq.answer: Very important. The About section adds personality and tells your story beyond technical skills. Share what drives you, what problems you enjoy solving, and what makes you unique. Keep it concise but authentic.

faq.question: Should I include my resume as a download?
faq.answer: Yes, offer a PDF resume download link, but do not make it the primary call to action. Your portfolio should showcase work visually first. The resume supplements the portfolio for hiring managers who want traditional documentation.

faq.question: How often should I update my portfolio?
faq.answer: Update your portfolio whenever you complete a significant project or learn a new skill. Review it quarterly to ensure all links work, projects reflect your current abilities, and the design feels fresh. Remove outdated or weaker projects as you add better work.
